            v = ((n ^ m) & high_mask) | ((1 ^ (n >> p & 1)) << p)
            if n ^ v > m:
                best = min(best, v)
    print(best)

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    # Find min v = x^n for x > m, i.e., x >= m+1
    p = m + 1
    # Find min (x^n) for x >= p
    # Try bit by bit
    bits = 30
    # Check if n >= p: then x=n gives x^n=0
    if n >= p:
        print(0)
        return
    # Otherwise find minimum x^n for x>=p
    best = float('inf')
    # Try setting a bit higher than p at position i, then set remaining bits = n
    for i in range(bits, -1, -1):
        # Force bit i of x to be higher than bit i of p, with prefix matching p above bit i
        # prefix of x matches p for bits > i, bit i of x > bit i of p
        # bit i of p must be 0 for this to work (so we can set it to 1)
        mask = (1 << (i+1)) - 1
        prefix = p & ~mask  # bits above i match p
        if (p >> i) & 1 == 0:
            x = prefix | (1 << i) | (n & (mask >> 1))
            best = min(best, x ^ n)
    print(best)

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    # Find smallest v >= 0 such that n^v > m
    # Equivalently, smallest v not in {n^0, n^1, ..., n^m}
    
    # Try each bit from high to low
    # We'll do it with bit manipulation
    
    # Find number of bits needed
    BITS = 30
    
    # Find smallest v such that n^v > m
    # Greedy: at each bit, try 0 first
    # Track: are we already "greater" (impossible since we want minimum)
    # Actually we want n^v > m, try to find min v
    
    result = 0
    # We'll try to construct v bit by bit
    # At each step, track if n^v is already determined to be > m (then remaining bits = 0)
    
    ans = None
    for bit in range(BITS, -1, -1):
        for vb in [0, 1]:
            xb = ((n >> bit) & 1) ^ vb
            mb = (m >> bit) & 1
            if xb > mb:
                # This works, set remaining bits of v to 0
                ans = result | (vb << bit)
                break
            elif xb < mb:
                # This bit makes n^v < m, skip
                continue
            else:
                # Equal so far, continue
                result |= (vb << bit)
                break
        if ans is not None:
            break
    
    if ans is None:
        ans = result  # n^ans == m, not > m... hmm
    
    print(ans)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
